questions.question: What type of variable is used to store text?
What Boolean value represents falsehood?
What symbol is commonly used to assign a value to a variable in most programming languages?
What programming construct is used to make decisions based on conditions?
What numeric data type is used to represent whole numbers without a fractional part?
What punctuation mark is often used at the end of a statement in languages like C, C++, and Java?
What do you call a named block of code designed to perform a specific task that can be reused?
What is the term for a mistake or flaw in a computer program that causes it to produce an incorrect or unexpected result?
What collection data type holds an ordered sequence of elements, often starting at index 0?
What type of data contains only two possible values, such as true or false?
What data structure operates on a Last-In, First-Out (LIFO) principle?
What data structure operates on a First-In, First-Out (FIFO) principle?
What OOP principle involves bundling the data and methods that operate on the data into a single unit?
What OOP principle allows a class to acquire the properties and methods of another class?
What OOP principle allows objects of different classes to be treated as objects of a common superclass?
What HTTP status code indicates that the server encountered an unexpected condition that prevented it from fulfilling the request?
What term describes a function that calls itself in order to solve a smaller instance of the same problem?
What acronym describes the four basic functions of persistent storage (Create, Read, Update, Delete)?
What widely used version control system was created by Linus Torvalds?
What is the process of automatically identifying and discarding objects that are no longer needed by a program to free up memory?
